referenced_section_hint: Securitisation — operational requirements for regulatory capital relief
description: APS 220 excludes from its scope exposures securitised or transferred into securitisation vehicles meeting APRA's operational requirements for regulatory capital relief under APS 120.
Terms defined in APS 001 appear in bold on first use in APS 220.

referenced_section_hint: Risk Management — overall risk appetite statement and risk management strategy
description: An ADI need not have a stand-alone credit risk appetite statement or strategy where they form part of the overall risk appetite statement or risk management strategy required under CPS 220.
An ADI that outsources aspects of credit risk management must have regard to CPS 231 for outsourced functions.
For large exposures, an ADI must also have regard to APS 221.
For associations with related entities, an ADI must also have regard to APS 222.

referenced_section_hint: Governance — foreign ADI senior officer with delegated authority
description: Board requirements for foreign ADIs must be read subject to CPS 510, which requires a foreign ADI to nominate a senior officer outside Australia with delegated authority.
In addition to requirements in CPS 220, APS 221 and APS 222, an ADI must develop policies to ensure adequate portfolio diversification.
Portfolio diversification requirements are in addition to requirements in APS 221.
Portfolio diversification requirements are in addition to requirements in APS 222.
Paragraph 46 refers to Attachment A for detailed requirements on collateral valuation policies and processes.

referenced_section_hint: Capital Adequacy: Counterparty Credit Risk — standardised approach or adjusted current exposure method
description: For derivative transaction exposures, an ADI must calculate exposures for non-performance measurement purposes under the standardised approach or adjusted current exposure method prescribed in APS 180.
Paragraphs 108-109 refer to Attachment B for the prescribed provisioning approach that APRA may permit or require an ADI to implement.

referenced_section_hint: eligible lenders mortgage insurer definition and eligible collateral provisions
description: Attachment B references APS 112 for the definition of eligible lenders mortgage insurer (Category One and Two exposures) and for eligible collateral provisions applicable to cash on deposit deductions (Category Three, paragraph 16(d)).
